name: api-design
description: REST API design, authentication, pagination, error handling, rate limiting, and OpenAPI patterns

entries.title: REST API Design Conventions
entries.content: Resource-oriented URLs: /users, /users/:id, /users/:id/posts. HTTP methods: GET (read), POST (create), PUT (full update), PATCH (partial update), DELETE (remove). Status codes: 200 OK, 201 Created, 204 No Content, 400 Bad Request, 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden, 404 Not Found, 409 Conflict, 422 Unprocessable, 429 Too Many, 500 Server Error. Always return JSON. Use plural nouns for resources. Nest for relationships (max 2 levels).

entries.title: JWT Authentication Pattern
entries.content: Issue access token (short-lived, 15min) + refresh token (long-lived, 7d). Store refresh token in httpOnly cookie. Access token in Authorization header. Rotate refresh tokens on use. Revocation via token blacklist or version counter.

entries.title: Pagination Patterns (Offset & Cursor)
entries.content: Offset pagination: ?page=2&per_page=20. Simple but slow on large datasets (OFFSET scales poorly). Cursor pagination: ?cursor=abc123&limit=20. Use indexed column (id, created_at) as cursor. Return nextCursor in response. Better for real-time data and infinite scroll.

entries.title: Rate Limiting with Sliding Window
entries.content: Protect APIs from abuse. Sliding window counter per IP/user. Return 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After header. Use Redis for distributed rate limiting. Implement in middleware.

entries.title: Consistent API Error Handling
entries.content: Use a global error handler middleware. Map error types to HTTP status codes. Return structured error objects. Log server errors, don't expose internals. Include request IDs for debugging.
